Problem:
Extend cows' bitstring IDs so no cow can be mistaken for another when some reported IDs are truncated.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

Node started: Given a set of N binary strings S = {s1, s2, ..., sN}, construct a set of extended binary strings E = {e1, e2, ..., eN} such that: 1. Extension Constraint: For every i in {1, ..., N}, si is a prefix of ei. 2. Prefix-Free Constraint: No string in E is a prefix of another string in E. That is, for all i != j, ei is not a prefix of ej. 3. Optimization Objective: Minimize the sequence of lengths (|e1|, |e2|, ..., |eN|) lexicographically. Note: The lexicographical minimization is applied to the vector of lengths. If multiple sets of lengths achieve the same minimum, any such set is valid, but the problem implies a unique minimal length vector for each si based on the construction requirements.
